lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Monica and Chandler are sitting on one of the chairs doing a crossword puzzle.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you guys doing up?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, we wanted to finish the crossword before we went to bed. Hey, do you know a six-letter word for red?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (thinks) Dark red.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I think that's wrong, but there's a Connect the Dots in here for you later. (To Monica) Hey, how about maroon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (checks to see if it works) Yes, you are so smart! (Kisses him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww, you guys are so cute!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'll see you in the morning.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Monica
lines.text: Okay.
Joey's bedroom, time lapse. He's asleep and dreaming. In his dream he's doing the crossword puzzle with...wait for it...Monica!

lines.speaker: Dream Monica
lines.text: Y'know, I love doing crossword puzzles with you honey!

lines.speaker: Dream Joey
lines.text: Aww, me too. Now let's finish this and go to bed.

lines.speaker: Dream Monica
lines.text: Okay! There's only one left, three letter word, not dog but...

lines.speaker: Dream Joey
lines.text: Cat.

lines.speaker: Dream Monica
lines.text: Yes! You are so smart! (Kisses him.) I love you.

lines.speaker: Dream Joey
lines.text: I love you too.
They hug.
[Cut back to Joey in bed, he's smiling, enjoying the dream as he wakes up. Suddenly, he realized what he was dreaming about and bolts upright in bed.]
Opening Credits
Central Perk, everyone is there but Ross and Joey. Gunther hands them the bill, and Chandler gives some money to pay it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looking at the bill) Uhh, we still need a tip.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. Hold on. (She starts digging in the chair.) I got it. Nickel! (Donates it.) How much more do we need?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: A couple of bucks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, dime! (Donates that.) You guys should probably keep talking; this could take a while. (Finds something else.) Oh no, wait! Look it! Whoa! (Looks at it.) Oh my God, this is a police badge!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's so cool! Why would a cop come in here though? They don't serve donuts. (No one laughs.) Y'know what actually, could you discover the badge again? I think I can come up with something better than that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe, I bet somebody's missing that badge.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I should probably take it back. Ooh, but you know what? While I'm at the police station, I could check their Ten Most Wanted lists because my friend Fritzy has been like number 11 forever, so this could be her year! (She crosses her fingers in hope.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey, you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Monica) Hey. That uh, that my sweatshirt?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yes, it is. I'm sorry I borrowed it, I was cold. I hope its okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well uh, it's just that uh, y'know if-if you're gonna be wearing someone's sweatshirt shouldn't it be your boyfriends--and I'm not him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'll give it back to you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no! No! I mean it's gonna be all smelling like Monica!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you saying I smell bad?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! No, you smell like a meadow. (Pause.) I'm sorry. (Runs to the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's with him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y'know what? The last time Joey went to a meadow, his mother was shot by a hunter.

A couch store, Ross is trying to decide on a new couch for his place. He has dragged Rachel along for the trip, and she's not too happy about it. Ross is sitting on it in different ways to see how it feels. He tries to just sit on it normally, and then he tries flopping on it. One thing about this couch, it's huge. It's like twice the size of a normal full size couch. Whoever designed this thing, needs help and fast.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (disgusted at Ross's antics) Ugh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Rachel) Yeah, I still don't know. (To the salesman who is hovering nearby) I'm sorry I just wanna make sure that I bought the right couch. I need a couch that says, "Kids welcome here." But that also says, (In a sexy voice) "Come here to me!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! You say that to kids?!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! No! No! The "Come here to me" is y'know for the ladies.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, honey, it's a nice couch. It's not a magic couch.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: You picked a great couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Yeah. Could you just sign right here please? (Hands him a clipboard.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, sure. Whoa-whoa, what's this? The delivery charge is almost as much as the couch!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait! No, that's ridiculous. Come on, he lives three blocks away!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, y'know what? I'll take it myself, thank you! (He signs the form and hands it back to the salesman.) All right Rach, let's go! (He picks up one end of the couch.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah! (She puts on her coat and turns around and sees Ross is expecting her to help.) (Laughing.) Are you kiddin'?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, come on it's only three blocks! And-and, it's not very heavy, try it! Come on! Come on!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Disgustedly she goes and tries to pick up the couch. Much to her amazement, she is successful.) Oh. Oh! I can do it!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: You two are really gonna enjoy that couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, we're uh, yeah we're not together. (He starts backing out of the store.)

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Ohh, okay. (Laughs.) Something didn't quite add up there. (Ross stops, walks back to talk to the salesman, and in the process pushes Rachel up against a wall.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Well you, her, I mean, she's very...y'know. And you're like...y'know.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not that it's any of your business, but we did go out.

lines.speaker: The Salesman
lines.text: Really? You two?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, I don't really want to be doing this right now. I am carrying a very heavy couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then tell him quickly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Ross) Fine! (To the salesman) We went out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not only did we go out, we did it 298 times!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!! Oh my--ugh!! You kept count?! You are such a loser!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A loser you did it with (To the salesman) 298 times!
Rachel pushes on the couch and pushes Ross out the door.
Outside of Central Perk, Phoebe is exiting and sees a woman put out her cigarette on a tree.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Oh! Ma'am? Excuse me, ma'am?

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You can't put your cigarette out on a tree!

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: Yeah I can, it worked real well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No but you shouldn't! Don't ever do that again.

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: I won't! (Turns away) Until I have my next cigarette.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hold it! (Grabs the badge) N.Y.P.D! Freeze punk!

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah that's right you are so busted. (To no one in particular.) Book 'em.

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: Who are you talking too?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Save it Red! Unless you wanna spend the night in the slammer, you apologize to the tree.

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: I am not going to apologize to a tree!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You apologize to the tree right now or I am calling for backup. (The woman calls her bluff.) (Screaming at no one in particular) Backup! Backup!!

lines.speaker: The Smoking Woman
lines.text: I-I'm sorry! Sorry.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, cancel backup! Cancel backup!
Ross's building's lobby, he and Rachel are about to attempt to take the couch upstairs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (Throws off the last cushion.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, didn't you say that there was an elevator in here?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh, yes I did but there isn't. Okay, here we go.

They start the attempt. Ross is going backwards and reaches the first landing. This staircase has three steps then a landing, makes a 90-degree turn, and has more steps before another landing and another 90-degree turn.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, go left. Left! Left! (The bottom of the couch is hitting the railing.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know what? There is no more left, left!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh okay, lift it straight up over your head! Straight up over your head! You can do it! You can do it! (She gets it lifted up and they make the first turn.) Okay. You got it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good-good-good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh!
She can't stay at the end as the couch rounds the turn so she shifts to the back corner of the couch and is at a 90-degree angle to it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, you got it right? You got it right? You got it?
She don't got it as the couch slips out of their grips and falls over the bottom railing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Any chance you think the couch looks good there?
Chandler and Joey's, Phoebe is telling Chandler and Monica how she fought crime in her own way with the badge she found.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ...so this guy was all (Mumbles.) And I'm all, Buffay, Homicide. (Flashes the badge.) It was just so cool!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (cooking something) Phoebe, you were supposed to take that back!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know but I'm having so much fun doing good deeds.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but impersonating a police officer is a serious thing. You could get arrested.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You could get arrested, right now! (Flashes the badge and they glare at her.) All right, yeah, I gotta take it back. I'm totally drunk with power. (She heads for the door just as Joey enters.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Joey) Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Hey! (Sees that Monica's there.) Oh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, Joe.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I didn't know you guys were going to be here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey, sweetie, taste this. (Holds out a spoon for him.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (backing away) What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What is going on with you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nothing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on! You've been acting strange all day!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! There is something. I kinda had a dream, (pause) but I don't want to talk about it. (Starts for his room.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa-what-what if Martin Luther King had said that? (Imitating what his famous speech would sound like.) I kinda have a dream! I don't want to talk about it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, it involved Monica.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You had a dream about a girl that I am seeing?! Oh, that is so cool! (To Monica) I can't tell you how many times I've dreamt about a girl that he was seeing. (Seeing Monica's stare.) (To Joey) Anyway we're talking about your dream. (To Monica) I love you. (To Joey) Your dream? (Leans in to listen closely.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't worry, there wasn't any sex in it or anything. I haven't dreamt about her like that since I found out about you two--ish.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What was the dream about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, okay. You were my girlfriend and we were doing the crossword puzzle. Y'know like you guys were doing last night. So, that's it. I'm in love with Monica and I'll be moving out.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, Joey! Joey! That doesn't mean that-that you're in love with me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It-it doesn't?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it can mean anything. Like uh, all of the sudden you're jealous because I've become the apartment stud.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That kinda sounds like your dream dude.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Or, it could mean that-that you saw Chandler and me together and we y'know were being close and stuff and then you just want to have that with someone too.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: In the dream I did enjoy the closeness.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Um-hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Joey, look, are you attracted to Monica? Right here, right now, are you attracted to her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looks at her) Not really.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well there you have it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well sure! I'm just wearing sweats! (Looking at Chandler and slowly realizing what his point is.) But that's good that you're not in love with me, because you just want a girlfriend!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I don't think it's just about just getting a girlfriend. Y'know? I mean, yeah, I can get a girlfriend! Yeah, we could sit in the chair and do crosswords, but y'know are we ever going to have y'know the closeness like-like you guys have?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well y'know, Monica and I were friends before we started dating. So maybe-maybe that's it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Friends first? That's interesting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You become friends after?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, never done that either.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey, umm, do you guys have that tape measure?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, it's actually in my bedroom.
Monica and Chandler both remember a special moment between them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughing) That's right.
They realize the implication of their behavior, stop instantly and head for his bedroom. In the meanwhile, Joey is starring at Rachel in a seductive way.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (noticing him) What's up Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in a sexy voice) How you doin'?
Rachel is stunned.
Commercial Break
Outside Central Perk, Phoebe is walking up and notices a car that is parked half on the curb and right in front of the door, making it difficult for people to enter Central Perk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Excuse me, is this your car?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well I don't think it's very nice of you to park here, y'know you're blocking the entrance.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Don't worry about it. It's not a problem.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it's a problem for me, which means it's a problem for you 'cause I'm a cop. (Shows the badge.)

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: (he reaches into the car and slams his siren on the roof.) So am I!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, no. (Pause) Oh okay, so you're a cop which means you can park anywhere, 'cause I know that 'cause I'm a cop too. So, all right, keep up the good work. 10-4. (Tries to leave.)

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: (stopping her) Hey, wait a second! So wait, what precinct are you with?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I'm with the umm, the 57th.

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Oh, I know a guy in homicide up there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm in vice. Yeah, in fact I'm undercover right now. I'm a whore.

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Who-who else is in vice up there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, do you know, umm Sipowicz?

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Sipowicz? No, I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, big guy, kinda bald.

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: No, I don't know him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (starts to walk away, but stops) Don't try to call him or anything, 'cause he's not there, he's out. His umm, his partner just died.

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Wow umm, tell Sipowicz I'm real sorry for his loss.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I sure will, take care. (Starts walking off.)

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: (following her) Hey by the way, I'm sure Sipowicz is gonna be all right. I heard that kid from Silver Spoons is really good. (Phoebe's stunned) And where did you find my badge?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. (She starts laughing. Then she throws the badge at him and runs away.)
Monica and Rachel's, Joey is entering. Rachel is there getting some tools to help Ross out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Joey, would you mind giving me and Ross a hand moving his couch?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, I'd love too, but I got acting class. But y'know what? I guess I can blow that off, (In a sexy voice) for you.
He starts staring at her longingly.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, hey, Rach let me ask you something. Uh, I was just over there talking to Monica and Chandler, boy they are really tight.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah that's not such a bad situation they got going over there. I'm thinking of getting me one of those.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's up Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, the reason I think Monica and Chandler are so great...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: ...is because they were friends first. Y'know? So I asked myself, "Who are my friends?" You and Phoebe, and I saw you first. So...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughing) What are you saying?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm saying maybe you and I crank it up a notch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Y'know honey, umm, as uh, as flattered as I am that uh, you saw me first, uhh, I just, I-I don't think we should be cranking anything up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'll treat you real nice. (Pulls out a chair for her.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs and pushes the chair back in) Yeah, well, y'know umm... No honey, listen I think it's a great idea to become friends with someone before you date them, but I think the way you do it is y'know you meet someone, become their friend, build a foundation, then you ask them out on a date. Don't hit on your existing friends!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Won't-won't that take longer?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah. Oh, but once you find it, ohh it's so worth the wait.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. I understand. I understand. (Pause) Man, I wish I saw Phoebe first!
The lobby of Ross's building, he's sitting on the couch at the bottom of the stairs, and he's practicing enticing women to join him on the couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here to me. No-no, you come here to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey Ross! I brought reinforcements.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great! What, you brought Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I brought the next best thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler?! You brought Chandler?! The next best thing would be Monica!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I would be offended, but Monica is freakishly strong, so...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I-I drew a sketch about how we're gonna do it. (Showing them) Okay Rach, (points to the sketch) that's you. That's the couch. (Points again.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa-oh, what's-what's that? (Points.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that's me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! You certainly think a lot of yourself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! That's-that's my arm!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looking at the sketch) Oh, I see. I thought you just really, really liked your new couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what? Just-just follow my lead.
Chandler and Ross head for opposite ends of the couch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, Chandler.
They pick up the couch and after throwing off the last pillow; Rachel helps out on Chandler's end.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right. (They start up the stairs. Ross is first.) Okay, here we go!
Chandler has moved forward and is now underneath the couch as it heads up the first set of stairs.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Turn.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (straining) Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Turn! Turn!
As they turn the couch, Chandler gets sandwiched between the railing and the couch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I don't think we can turn anymore!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, I don't, I just don't think it's going to fit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah it will! Come on, up! Up-up-up! Up! Yes! Here we go! Pivot! (They start up the stairs again. Chandler is between the couch and the wall now.) Pivot! Piv-ot! Piv-et!! Piv-ett!!! Piv-et!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Shut up! Shut up! Shut up!!
They set the couch down.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I don't think it's going to pivot anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Rachel
lines.text: You think?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, let's uh, let's bring it back down and-and try again.
As they start back down the couch drops a little bit and gets jammed. They try to free it to no avail.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, yeah, I think it's really stuck now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe that didn't work!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, me neither! I mean, you had a sketch!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y'know, what did you mean when you said pivot?
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is returning and finds Chandler, Monica, and Rachel are there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! How's it going? Did you make any new friends?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, I met this woman. (Starts for his room.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (stopping him) Hey, whoa-whoa! What's she like?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, well, she's...really good in bed.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, I thought you were gonna try to be friends first!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Rachel) Well look, hey, it's all your fault!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well because you didn't give me advice! No! You gave me a pickup line! As soon as I told her I wanted to y'know, build a foundation and be friends first. I suddenly, through no fault of my own, became irresistible to her! (Pause) And her roommate!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about the closeness?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Closeness-shmoshness! There was three of us for crying out loud!
Monica and Rachel's, Joey is entering with a pizza and finds everyone but Ross there.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) All right! Hey, who wants pizza?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, I do! I do! I do!
They all walk over to get a slice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (taking a bite) Oh, great! Can you believe I found it on the second floor?
They all throw their pieces back as there is a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who is it?

lines.speaker: Voice
lines.text: N.Y.P.D!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe and Joey
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, just a minute officer!!

He throws his piece back in the box, runs into the living room, looks for a place to hide the pizza, finds one, slides the box under the couch, sits down on the table, and tries to quickly chew the food in his mouth.

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: I'm looking for Phoebe Buffay!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, God, it's him! It's that cop! God, I can't believe it! He found me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God, Phoebe, are you gonna go to jail?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if I'm going down, I'm taking you (Points at all of them) with me. (They all look at her.) Harboring a fugitive? That's one to three years minimum. Good luck Chandler. (She opens the door to the cop from before.) Okay, you can arrest me. Fine. But you'll never make it stick and you know it!

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Yeah, but I kinda don't have a choice, it's my job. I mean, you understand right?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep! As long as you understand that I'm going to call my lawyer and once he puts you on the stand he'll make you look like a fool. A fool!

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: I don't like looking foolish. Y'know what? Maybe uh, I don't arrest you today. Maybe I came by and you weren't here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I would love it if I weren't here!

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Okay, so since umm, you're not going to jail tonight I was wondering if you would like to go to dinner with me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Me?!

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Yeah. Ever since you flashed my badge at me, I kinda can't stop thinking about you. You're the prettiest, fake undercover whore I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: Nice!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! I didn't see that coming! You're-you're asking me out!

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Yeah. I mean, I coulda done it better, but these people keep staring at me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, yeah, I'd like to go out with you officer...

lines.speaker: Cop
lines.text: Gary.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Gary.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay, so it's a date.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! So--ooh, I gotta ask you though. How did you know where to find me?

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Well you're fingerprints were all over my badge so I just ran it through the computer and this was listed as your last known address so I just checked it out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, impressive.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Not as impressive as you. I gotta tell you, I looked at your record and you've done some pretty weird stuff.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, we'll talk at dinner.

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay. (He starts to leave.) So I'll come by in a couple hours and pick you up?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I can't wait!

lines.speaker: Gary
lines.text: Okay. And don't worry, I'm not just gonna take you out for donuts.
Chandler busts out laughing and everyone just looks at him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Rachel) He has a gun!
Closing Credits
The couch store, Ross is talking to a saleswoman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'd like to return this couch. I'm not satisfied with it.
[The camera cuts to show the couch, which has been cut in half.]

lines.speaker: The Saleswoman
lines.text: You wanna return this couch? (Ross nods yes.) It's cut in half!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's what I'm telling you.

lines.speaker: The Saleswoman
lines.text: Did you cut this couch in half?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This couch, is cut in half! I would like to exchange it for one that is not cut in half!

lines.speaker: The Saleswoman
lines.text: You're telling me this couch was delivered to you like this?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, I am a reasonable man. I will accept store credit.

lines.speaker: The Saleswoman
lines.text: I'll give you store credit in the amount of four dollars.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (thinks) I take it.
End
